title_en: Al-Kawthari: Defender of the Kalam Tradition
content_en:
Muhammad Zahid al-Kawthari (1296–1371 AH / 1879–1951 CE) was one of the most distinguished and controversial Islamic scholars of the modern period. Born in the Caucasus region and educated in Istanbul in the Ottoman scholarly tradition, he rose to become the deputy (naibu l-mashikhah) of the Ottoman Shaykh al-Islam — the highest religious office in the Ottoman state — before the empire's collapse. After the abolition of the Caliphate in 1924, he left Turkey and eventually settled in Egypt, where he spent the last decades of his life as a scholar, editor, and writer.

Al-Kawthari was a formidable polemicist in defense of the Hanafi-Maturidi theological and legal tradition. He wrote extensively against what he regarded as the theological errors of Ibn Taymiyyah and the Hanbali Athari school, and he was deeply committed to the Ash'ari and Maturidi kalam traditions as the authentic expression of Sunni theology. His scholarly output included critical editions of classical texts, biographical studies, hadith evaluations, and extensive polemical correspondence with scholars who held Athari positions.

An-Nasihah al-Kafiyah li-man Yatawalla Dawlat as-Sunniyyah — The Sufficient Counsel for Those Who Take Charge of the Sunni State — is al-Kawthari's major work in defense of the Ash'ari and Maturidi theological traditions against the rising influence of what he regarded as Athari theological innovation. The title's reference to those who take charge of the Sunni state reflects the political urgency he felt: as the Ottoman state was dissolving and new political configurations were emerging across the Muslim world, al-Kawthari was concerned about which theological tradition would shape the religious character of the Muslim polities that would follow.

The work is both a positive defense of the Ash'ari and Maturidi traditions and a critique of the Athari positions associated with Ibn Taymiyyah and his followers. Al-Kawthari argued that the Athari approach to divine attributes, particularly the affirmation of divine attributes in their apparent sense without interpretation, constituted a form of anthropomorphism that contradicted authentic Sunni theology.
content_id:
Muhammad Zahid al-Kawthari (1296-1371 H / 1879-1951 M) adalah salah satu ulama Islam paling terkemuka sekaligus paling kontroversial di era modern. Lahir di kawasan Kaukasus dan menempuh pendidikan di Istanbul dalam tradisi keilmuan Ottoman, beliau naik menjadi wakil (naib al-mashikhah) Syaikh al-Islam Ottoman yaitu jabatan keagamaan tertinggi dalam negara Ottoman sebelum keruntuhan kekaisaran tersebut. Setelah penghapusan Kekhalifahan pada tahun 1924, beliau meninggalkan Turki dan akhirnya menetap di Mesir, di mana beliau menghabiskan beberapa dekade terakhir hidupnya sebagai ulama, editor, dan penulis yang sangat produktif.

Al-Kawthari adalah polemikus tangguh dalam membela tradisi teologi dan fikih Hanafi-Maturidi. Beliau menulis secara ekstensif menentang apa yang dianggapnya sebagai kesalahan-kesalahan teologi Ibn Taymiyyah dan mazhab Athari Hanbali, dan beliau sangat berkomitmen pada tradisi ilmu kalam Ash`ari dan Maturidi sebagai ekspresi otentik aqidah Sunni. Karya keilmuannya mencakup edisi kritis teks-teks klasik, kajian biografi, evaluasi hadis, dan korespondensi polemis yang ekstensif dengan para ulama yang berpegang pada posisi Athari.

An-Nasihah al-Kafiyah adalah karya utama al-Kawthari dalam membela tradisi teologi Ash`ari dan Maturidi terhadap pengaruh yang berkembang dari apa yang beliau anggap sebagai inovasi teologi Athari. Judul karya ini mencerminkan urgensi politik yang beliau rasakan: ketika negara Ottoman sedang bubar dan konfigurasi politik baru bermunculan di seluruh dunia Islam, al-Kawthari khawatir tentang tradisi teologi mana yang akan membentuk karakter keagamaan negara-negara Muslim yang akan datang.

Karya ini sekaligus merupakan pembelaan positif terhadap tradisi Ash`ari dan Maturidi serta kritik terhadap posisi-posisi Athari yang dikaitkan dengan Ibn Taymiyyah dan para pengikutnya. Al-Kawthari berpendapat bahwa pendekatan Athari terhadap sifat-sifat ilahi, khususnya penegasan sifat-sifat ilahi dalam pengertian zahirnya tanpa takwil, merupakan bentuk tasybih yang bertentangan dengan aqidah Sunni yang otentik.
